Conversion is separating from the ways and habits of this world, dedicating ourselves to the behavior directed by the word of God. When we commit ourselves to doing the commandments of God, it is to do all his commandments, and to look to him as Lord, our Lord.

Having made promises against the sins they had committed, they obligated themselves to observe the duties they had neglected. We must not only stop doing evil, but learn to do good. Let no one expect the blessing of God unless they maintain public worship. It is likely to go well with our homes when care is taken that the work of God’s house goes on well. When everyone helps and everyone gives, even if it is just a little, toward a good work, the total will become a large sum. We must do what we can in works of piety and charity; and whatever situation we are placed in, cheerfully perform our duty to God, which will be the surest way to ease and liberty. As the ordinances of God are the appointed means of support for our souls, the believer will not begrudge the expense; yet most people leave their souls to starve.
